name: Shaky Isles South
description: A South Island long route with extended distance and changing weather exposure.
difficultyExplanation: This South Island half combines backcountry exposure, sustained elevation, and variable weather windows. Strong route management is needed when towns and refuelling options are far apart.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Haast Pass corridor is exposed and the Mackenzie Basin approach can see brutal crosswinds. Time your passage for stable weather.
routeSummary: Shaky Isles South is a hard bikepacking route covering about 1,295 km, commonly ridden over 9 days. A South Island long route with extended distance and changing weather exposure. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Oct-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Haast Pass corridor is exposed and the Mackenzie Basin approach can see brutal crosswinds. Time your passage for stable weather. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 9-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
